fields.description:
## Why
Three lightweight, ADVISORY/observational reliability additions from the dso gap report (#7 FP
ledger, #10 grounding-health telemetry, #12 approach-viability note). None ever changes the
deterministic verdict or adds a BLOCK source; all are coverage/coaching signals for agent consumers.

## What
Concrete decisions (resolving the plan-review advisories â€” thresholds named, data-flow specified):

### #7 â€” fp:code-review ledger + compile step
- Convention doc `docs/code-review-fp-ledger.md` (the concrete artifact): a confirmed false
  positive / invalid finding from the code-review gate is recorded as a `bug`-typed rebar ticket
  tagged `fp:code-review`, whose body carries REQUIRED fields â€” the finding (criterion + finding
  text), the diff/context that triggered it, and a `root-cause` drawn from a CLOSED enum:
  `false-evidence`, `rubric-overapplication`, `hallucinated-gap`, `scope-mismatch`, `stale-baseline`.
- Light compile tooling `src/rebar/llm/code_review/fp_ledger.py`:
  `compile_fp_ledger(repo_root) -> list[dict]` reads open `fp:code-review`-tagged tickets and DRAFTS
  no-fire eval cases in the EXISTING eval-spec dataset shape (`{id, corpus: "fp-ledger",
  expect: "pass", mode: <root-cause>, diff: <context>}`, matching code-review-*.eval.yaml). It is
  IDEMPOTENT: a drafted ticket gets a `compiled` tag and is skipped on re-run (processed-cursor via
  tag). Per-ticket ERROR ISOLATION: a malformed/unparseable ticket is skipped + logged, never aborts
  the batch, and a rebar-store error surfaces as an empty draft-list (never raises into a caller).
  MANUAL to start (Rule-of-Three: the corpus feeds evals once â‰Ą1 ticket exists; no auto-schedule).

### #10 â€” grounding-health telemetry + low-grounding note
- Named constant `NON_TRIVIAL_DIFF_LINES = 20` (module-level in `fp_ledger.py`'s sibling telemetry
  home / gate_dispatch): a diff is NON-TRIVIAL iff `changed_lines > NON_TRIVIAL_DIFF_LINES` OR
  `changed_files > 1`. Single decided value â€” not two open options.
- Extend `_attach_code_review_metrics` (gate_dispatch.py) `coverage["metrics"]` with:
  `findings_per_run` (len(blocking)+len(advisory)), `verify_requests` (sum of the agentic Pass-2
  verify step's `_usage.requests` â€” the SAME mechanism plan-review's `verify_requests` already uses,
  so per-agentic-pass request/tool-call counts ARE available), and `grounding_health` band
  (`"ok"` | `"low"`).
- LOW-GROUNDING NOTE: when the diff is NON-TRIVIAL and the agentic verify pass made ZERO requests
  (`verify_requests == 0`), set `grounding_health = "low"` and emit an advisory
  `coverage["grounding_note"]` naming the named threshold. NEVER blocks.

### #12 â€” approach-viability advisory note
- Named constants `MIN_SURVIVING_HIGH_PRIORITY = 3`, `MAX_PASS2_DROP_RATE = 0.5`.
- Deterministic post-Pass-3 signal (computed in `_attach_code_review_metrics` from the assembled
  verdict + recorder, so it is verdict-independent): if the surviving high-priority advisory count
  `>= MIN_SURVIVING_HIGH_PRIORITY` OR the Pass-2 drop rate `>= MAX_PASS2_DROP_RATE`
  (dropped / (dropped+surfaced+blocking), 0 when denominator 0), emit an advisory
  `coverage["approach_viability_note"]` for agent consumers. NEVER changes the verdict.

## Scope
`src/rebar/llm/code_review/fp_ledger.py` (new: constants + compile step),
`src/rebar/llm/workflow/gate_dispatch.py` (`_attach_code_review_metrics` enrichment + the two notes),
`docs/code-review-fp-ledger.md` (new). Unit tests for each. All advisory/observational â€” no
verdict-path change, no new BLOCK source.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `docs/code-review-fp-ledger.md` exists documenting: the `fp:code-review` tag, the required
      body fields, and the CLOSED `root-cause` enum (five values above).
- [ ] `compile_fp_ledger` drafts no-fire eval cases (existing fixture shape) from `fp:code-review`
      tickets, is idempotent (skips `compiled`-tagged), and isolates per-ticket errors â€” a unit test
      pins draft-shape, idempotency, and that a malformed ticket is skipped not raised.
- [ ] `coverage["metrics"]` carries `findings_per_run`, `verify_requests`, and `grounding_health`;
      the low-grounding note fires ONLY on a non-trivial diff with zero verify requests, using the
      named `NON_TRIVIAL_DIFF_LINES` constant; it never blocks (unit test).
- [ ] The approach-viability note fires on the named `MIN_SURVIVING_HIGH_PRIORITY` /
      `MAX_PASS2_DROP_RATE` thresholds and never changes the verdict (unit test asserts verdict
      unchanged with/without the note).
